channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump’s War in Iran, and Rising Gas Prices, Collide With Midterm Agenda
item.description: The attack on Iran has led to a surge in energy prices at a moment when the cost of living is a major issue heading into the fall elections.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: A tanker and a cargo ship in Muscat, Oman, on Monday. The war in Iran has affected the shipping of oil through the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman.

item.title: Oil Prices Will Remain High as Iran War Continues
item.description: While officials look for ways to ease oil shocks, experts say higher prices will likely persist until traffic through the Strait of Hormuz returns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Black smoke filled the skies above Tehran on Sunday morning after Israeli airstrikes struck energy facilities overnight.

item.title: Missile in Deadly Iranian School Strike Appears to Be U.S.-Made, Photos Taken by Iran Show
item.description: Iranian state media posted mangled remnants it claims were from the Feb. 28 attack in Minab. An analysis shows they have the markings of a missile made by American manufacturers
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Chaos on Mamdani’s Doorstep: ‘We’ve Never Had Anything Like This Here’
item.description: For two hours, the streets around Gracie Mansion became the stage for a heated protest, reflecting a nation seething with angst and unease.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: A man is detained after he was seen throwing an incendiary device at a protest near Gracie Mansion in Manhattan on Saturday.

item.title: Mamdani Chooses His Words Carefully After Alleged Terror Attack
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been spare in his remarks following the attempted bombing at Gracie Mansion and arrests of two men who said they were motivated by ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been somewhat reserved in his comments following an attack outside Gracie Mansion, issuing two short statements and answering few questions.

item.title: Homemade Bombs in ISIS-Inspired Attack Were Deadly, N.Y.P.D. Says
item.description: Prosecutors say one of the two men who were arrested hoped the attack would be deadlier than the Boston Marathon bombing. Officials said they are seeing increasing signs of radicalization.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: Police Department bomb squad officers convened near Gracie Mansion on Sunday.

item.title: Alexander Brothers Found Guilty on All Counts in Sex-Trafficking Trial
item.description: The verdict comes more than a month after the trial began in Federal District Court in Manhattan where the jury heard weeks of emotional and often graphic testimony.
item.dc:creator: Debra Kamin, Kate Christobek and Benjamin Weiser
item.media:credit: REUTERS/Kent Edwards
item.media:description: Prosecutors say the Alexander brothers operated a sex trafficking conspiracy for nearly 20 years. Their trial ended on Monday, when the jury found them guilty on every count they each faced.

item.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, The Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
item.description: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire and Sajal Pradhan
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: Two Supreme Court Justices Debate Handling of Trump Emergency Cases
item.description: In a rare joint appearance, Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett M. Kavanaugh offered sharply different views on how the court should handle emergency requests.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, left, moderator Judge Paul L. Friedman, center, and Supreme Court Justice Brett M. Kavanaugh on Monday, where the two justices differed over how the court is handling emergency requests.

item.title: In Scathing Ruling, Judge Says 3 Trump Prosecutors Are in Unlawful Roles
item.description: The ruling found that the three-person leadership team in New Jersey’s federal prosecutor’s office was illegal, and sharply questioned President Trump’s priorities.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Since Alina Habba left office, three attorneys have been running the U.S. attorney’s office in New Jersey.

item.title: U.S. Solar Installations Fell in 2025 as Trump Attacked Clean Energy
item.description: More solar energy was added to U.S. grids than any other technology, but the amount installed fell by 14 percent, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Mason Trinca for The New York Times
item.media:description: A solar field in Riesel, Texas, in 2023. Solar power is projected to account for just over half of the new power projects that will be added this year.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Crowd Out Republicans’ Midterm Message
item.description: As the G.O.P. gathered in Miami for a party retreat where lawmakers hoped to focus on the economy, the president threatened to block his own party’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Polls suggest that Republicans’ chances of keeping control of the House are growing more slim.

item.title: Talent Agent Ari Emanuel Launches His Own Podcast, ‘Rushmore’
item.description: Hollywood’s archetypical agent has been showing a very different side of himself as a host of a video podcast called “Rushmore.”
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.media:credit: Doug Chayka

item.title: O.S.U. President Walter Carter Jr. Resigns Over ‘Inappropriate Relationship’
item.description: The university said it was investigating a company owned by a podcaster after the president, Walter Carter Jr., said he had “made a mistake.”
item.dc:creator: Vimal Patel
item.media:credit: Jason Mowry/Getty Images
item.media:description: Ohio State University president Walter Carter Jr. at Ohio Stadium in Columbus last year.

item.title: Billionaires Made 19% of Federal Election Campaign Contributions in 2024
item.description: Billionaires made 19 percent of all reported federal campaign contributions in 2024, a Times analysis shows, and even more in some local elections. Wealthy donors are reaping the rewards.
item.dc:creator: Mike Baker and Steven Rich
item.category: Citizens United v Federal Election Commission (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:credit: Win McNamee/Getty Images
item.media:description: Protesters demonstrated outside the Supreme Court in 2019 as Senate Democrats proposed a constitutional amendment to overturn the court’s decision that lifted many campaign finance restrictions.

item.title: 2 Teen Mariachi Musicians Released From ICE Detention
item.description: Representative Joaquin Castro of Texas led a delegation of Democrats to a South Texas detention center to press for the release of the brothers and their family.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Joaquin Castro, Democrat of Texas, greeting Caleb Gámez-Cuéllar and his family after their release from the Dilley Immigration Processing Center in Dilley, Texas, on Monday.

item.title: Locals in Other States Prepare for ICE With Lots of Rumors and Little Information
item.description: After the sprawling and chaotic federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota, jurisdictions in other states have been bracing for a range of scenarios.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The clashes during immigration sweeps in Minneapolis earlier this year have left local activists and officials in other cities worried that their communities could face similar crackdowns.

item.title: Epstein’s Remote Zorro Ranch Is Searched by New Mexico Investigators
item.description: Some of the disgraced financier’s victims have said they were trafficked at the property south of Santa Fe, where the nearest neighbors are miles away.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lawmakers voted to impanel a bipartisan four-member “truth commission” with subpoena power to investigate Zorro Ranch.

item.title: Casey Wasserman Agency Removes His Name From Company in Epstein Fallout
item.description: The sports and marketing agency is contending with the fallout from the Epstein files, and it is continuing to look for a buyer.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Nicole Stock
item.category: Los Angeles Organizing Committee for the Olympic and Paralympic Games 2028 (LA28)
item.media:credit: Guglielmo Mangiapane/Reuters
item.media:description: Casey Wasserman suffered a reputational blow early this year after files released by the Justice Department included suggestive emails he sent years ago to the longtime companion of Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: A Night Light in the Sky? Reflect Orbital Wants to Launch a Big Space Mirror.
item.description: The company is seeking F.C.C. approval to test an idea to reflect sunlight to Earth at night, possibly powering solar panels. Critics say it could be bad for people and wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang and Hiroko Tabuchi
item.media:credit: Tim Gruber for The New York Times
item.media:description: The start-up Reflect Orbital wants to launch mirror-bearing satellites which it says could address one of the biggest weaknesses of solar power: that electric generation stops when the sun goes down.

item.title: Trump Has No Idea How to End the War With Iran
item.description: Only when the U.S.-Israeli bombing of Iran comes to a halt, without destroying the state, can the Iranian people sort out their fate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times

item.title: Latin Americans Already Have a Serious Partner — and It’s Not Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to force the Western Hemisphere into alignment will keep falling short.
item.dc:creator: Oliver Stuenkel
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Philotheus Nisch for The New York Times; source photograph by Eric Lee, via The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Scolds Trump Over Gasoline Prices
item.description: “You can lie about many things in American life, but one thing you can’t lie about is gas prices,” Late Night host Seth Meyers said. “Everyone sees it.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: Bo Gritz, Vietnam Veteran Called a Real-Life Rambo, Dies at 87
item.description: He served in the Special Forces, led a postwar raid to find P.O.W.s and became a voice of the right-wing anti-government fringe.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: John Storey/Getty Images
item.media:description: Bo Gritz in the early 1990s. As a prominent figure among right-wing radicals, he tapped into a rich vein of populist anger over America’s defeat in Vietnam.

item.title: Meteorite Crashes Through Roof in Germany After Fiery Light Show
item.description: The fireball from space was spotted by a network of sky-watching cameras in Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and Germany.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

item.title: Lawmakers Pressure Hochul to Raise Taxes in New York
item.description: The State Assembly and Senate are each calling for the state to raise taxes, piggybacking on a push by Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul and Mayor Zohran Mamdani have become allies, but she has resisted his calls to raise taxes on the rich.

item.title: An Amish Avatar and an A.I. Monk Are Pitching Supplements on Social Media
item.description: Influencers have long hawked supplements on platforms like Instagram and TikTok. Increasingly, the influencers are fake.
item.dc:creator: Ken Bensinger, Tiffany Hsu and Qilai Shen
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
